mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and improving aggregate supply, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings benefit from increased production and operating leverage.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns is negative or statically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.1), or the correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent quarterly returns is below 0.10 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days as optimism about continued expansion prevails.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for at least one monthly observation
failure_condition: In more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative, or the average return difference between such episodes and control periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 3% after a prolonged period below 2% signals the onset of monetary tightening, which increases the discount rate on future earnings and depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3% from a level below 2% within the previous 12 months
failure_condition: Following the crossing threshold, more than 40% of 20-day forward returns are positive, or the cumulative excess return over a risk-free benchmark is negative over a 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales over at least two consecutive months signal robust consumer and construction demand, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS register month-over-month increases for at least 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: expansion phase of the business cycle
failure_condition: When the joint trigger is active, the average 60-day forward return is negative, or the risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio) is lower than during non-triggered periods with a p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in both nonfarm payrolls and industrial production over three months indicate synchronized strength across labor and manufacturing, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and lifting equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase month-over-month for at least 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: In episodes with three consecutive joint increases, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided Wilcoxon test, p > 0.1), or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in consumer price inflation, defined as two consecutive months of month-over-month CPI growth exceeding 0.5%, signals overheating and anticipated Federal Reserve tightening, which creates downward pressure on equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of events, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When annual GDP growth is below the long-term average (between 0% and 2.5%), a rise in housing starts signals that the demand-sensitive construction sector is leading a recovery, boosting economic sentiment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is between 0% and 2.5% (based on most recent quarterly release) and HOUST increases month-over-month
failure_condition: When the regime condition holds and HOUST rises, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) relative to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a period when nonfarm payrolls are below their 12-month moving average, a positive month-over-month change signals that the labor market is bottoming and about to enter an upswing, leading to favorable equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal direction is positive only in this low-trend regime; when payrolls are above trend the same change would be interpreted negatively as late-cycle risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is below its 12-month simple moving average
failure_condition: When PAYEMS is below its 12-month MA and increases, the 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly greater than the mean return when PAYEMS is above its MA (two-sample t-test, p > 0.05).
